lines.gurmukhi: ਤੀਰਥਿ ਨਾਈਐ ਸੁਖੁ ਫਲੁ ਪਾਈਐ ਮੈਲੁ ਨ ਲਾਗੈ ਕਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: teerath naaeeai sukh fal paaeeai mail na laagai kaaee |
lines.english: We bathe at sacred shrines of pilgrimage, and obtain the fruits of peace; not even an iota of filth sticks to us.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੋਰਖ ਪੂਤੁ ਲੋਹਾਰੀਪਾ ਬੋਲੈ ਜੋਗ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਬਿਧਿ ਸਾਈ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: gorakh poot lohaareepaa bolai jog jugat bidh saaee |7|
lines.english: Luhaareepaa, the disciple of Gorakh says, this is the Way of Yoga." ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਾਟੀ ਬਾਟੀ ਨੀਦ ਨ ਆਵੈ ਪਰ ਘਰਿ ਚਿਤੁ ਨ ਡੋੁਲਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: haattee baattee need na aavai par ghar chit na ddoulaaee |
lines.english: In the stores and on the road, do not sleep; do not let your consciousness covet anyone else's home.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਾਟੁ ਪਟਣੁ ਘਰੁ ਗੁਰੂ ਦਿਖਾਇਆ ਸਹਜੇ ਸਚੁ ਵਾਪਾਰੋ ॥
lines.translit: haatt pattan ghar guroo dikhaaeaa sahaje sach vaapaaro |
lines.english: The Guru has revealed the stores and the city within the home of my own heart, where I intuitively carry on the true trade.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਮਨੁ ਟੇਕ ਨ ਟਿਕਈ ਨਾਨਕ ਭੂਖ ਨ ਜਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: bin naavai man ttek na ttikee naanak bhookh na jaaee |
lines.english: Without the Name, the mind has no firm support; O Nanak, this hunger never departs.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਰਸਨੁ ਭੇਖ ਕਰਹੁ ਜੋਗਿੰਦ੍ਰਾ ਮੁੰਦ੍ਰਾ ਝੋਲੀ ਖਿੰਥਾ ॥
lines.translit: darasan bhekh karahu jogindraa mundraa jholee khinthaa |
lines.english: "Wear the robes of the sect of Yogis who follow Gorakh; put on the ear-rings, begging wallet and patched coat.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖੰਡਿਤ ਨਿਦ੍ਰਾ ਅਲਪ ਅਹਾਰੰ ਨਾਨਕ ਤਤੁ ਬੀਚਾਰੋ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: khanddit nidraa alap ahaaran naanak tat beechaaro |8|
lines.english: Sleep little, and eat little; O Nanak, this is the essence of wisdom. ||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਰਹ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਏਕੁ ਸਰੇਵਹੁ ਖਟੁ ਦਰਸਨ ਇਕ ਪੰਥਾ ॥
lines.translit: baarah antar ek sarevahu khatt darasan ik panthaa |
lines.english: Among the twelve schools of Yoga, ours is the highest; among the six schools of philosophy, ours is the best path.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਨ ਬਿਧਿ ਮਨੁ ਸਮਝਾਈਐ ਪੁਰਖਾ ਬਾਹੁੜਿ ਚੋਟ ਨ ਖਾਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: ein bidh man samajhaaeeai purakhaa baahurr chott na khaaeeai |
lines.english: This is the way to instruct the mind, so you will never suffer beatings again."

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਸਬਦੁ ਨਿਰੰਤਰਿ ਮੁਦ੍ਰਾ ਹਉਮੈ ਮਮਤਾ ਦੂਰਿ ਕਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: antar sabad nirantar mudraa haumai mamataa door karee |
lines.english: Let constant absorption in the Word of the Shabad deep within be your ear-rings; eradicate egotism and attachment.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕੁ ਬੋਲੈ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਬੂਝੈ ਜੋਗ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਇਵ ਪਾਈਐ ॥੯॥
lines.translit: naanak bolai guramukh boojhai jog jugat iv paaeeai |9|
lines.english: Nanak speaks: the Gurmukh understands; this is the way that Yoga is attained. ||9||

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਿੰਥਾ ਝੋਲੀ ਭਰਿਪੁਰਿ ਰਹਿਆ ਨਾਨਕ ਤਾਰੈ ਏਕੁ ਹਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: khinthaa jholee bharipur rahiaa naanak taarai ek haree |
lines.english: For your patched coat and begging bowl, see the Lord God pervading and permeating everywhere; O Nanak, the One Lord will carry you across.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਮੁ ਕ੍ਰੋਧੁ ਅਹੰਕਾਰੁ ਨਿਵਾਰੈ ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਸੁ ਸਮਝ ਪਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: kaam krodh ahankaar nivaarai gur kai sabad su samajh paree |
lines.english: Discard sexual desire, anger and egotism, and through the Word of the Guru's Shabad, attain true understanding.
Let your mind turn away in detachment from the world, and let this be your begging bowl. Let the lessons of the five elements be your cap.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਚਾ ਸਾਹਿਬੁ ਸਾਚੀ ਨਾਈ ਪਰਖੈ ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਬਾਤ ਖਰੀ ॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: saachaa saahib saachee naaee parakhai gur kee baat kharee |10|
lines.english: True is our Lord and Master, and True is His Name. Analyze it, and you shall find the Word of the Guru to be True. ||10||
Let truth, contentment and self-discipline be your companions.
Let the body be your meditation mat, and the mind your loin cloth.
O Nanak, the Gurmukh dwells on the Naam, the Name of the Lord. ||11||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਵਨੁ ਸੁ ਗੁਪਤਾ ਕਵਨੁ ਸੁ ਮੁਕਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: kavan su gupataa kavan su mukataa |
lines.english: "Who is hidden? Who is liberated?
Who is united, inwardly and outwardly?

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਵਨੁ ਸੁ ਆਵੈ ਕਵਨੁ ਸੁ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: kavan su aavai kavan su jaae |
lines.english: Who comes, and who goes?
He is hidden within each and every heart. The Gurmukh is liberated.
Who is permeating and pervading the three worlds?" ||12||
Through the Word of the Shabad, one is united, inwardly and outwardly.
"How is one placed in bondage, and consumed by the serpent of Maya?
O Nanak, the Gurmukh merges in Truth. ||13||
The self-willed manmukh perishes, and comes and goes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਤਤੁ ਬੀਚਾਰੈ ਸੁ ਗੁਰੂ ਹਮਾਰਾ ॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: eihu tat beechaarai su guroo hamaaraa |14|
lines.english: One who understands this essence of reality is our Guru." ||14||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਉ ਕਰਿ ਨਿਰਮਲੁ ਕਿਉ ਕਰਿ ਅੰਧਿਆਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: kiau kar niramal kiau kar andhiaaraa |
lines.english: How does one become immaculate and pure? How is the darkness of ignorance removed?

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਉ ਕਰਿ ਖੋਇਆ ਕਿਉ ਕਰਿ ਲਾਧਾ ॥
lines.translit: kiau kar khoeaa kiau kar laadhaa |
lines.english: How does one lose, and how does one gain?
The self-willed manmukh loses, and the Gurmukh gains.
Man is bound by evil-mindedness, and consumed by Maya, the serpent.
Meeting the True Guru, darkness is dispelled.
Focused deep within, in perfect absorption,
O Nanak, eradicating egotism, one merges in the Lord. ||15||

lines.gurmukhi: ਉਡੈ ਨ ਹੰਸਾ ਪੜੈ ਨ ਕੰਧੁ ॥
lines.translit: auddai na hansaa parrai na kandh |
lines.english: the soul-swan does not fly away, and the body-wall does not collapse.
Then, one knows that his true home is in the cave of intuitive poise.
"Why have you left your house and become a wandering Udaasee?
O Nanak, the True Lord loves those who are truthful. ||16||
What merchandise do you trade?
How will you carry others across with you?" ||17||
Why have you adopted these religious robes?
I became a wandering Udaasee, searching for the Gurmukhs.
I have adopted these robes seeking the Blessed Vision of the Lord's Darshan.
I trade in the merchandise of Truth.
"How have you changed the course of your life?

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਹੇ ਕਉ ਤੁਝੁ ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਲਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: kaahe kau tujh ihu man laaeaa |
lines.english: With what have you linked your mind?
O Nanak, as Gurmukh, I carry others across. ||18||
